title: Misnamed directly nested generate blocks
body:
Hi,

There is a problem to reference memory arrays in demo TB of the swerv EH2/EL2 blocks. 
See email thread below.

> You can use the same RTL, just change your DRAM define as below.
> Verilator seems to add genblk's where other simulators don't,
> unfortunately this isn't a well specified area of the language. Please
> file a bug on Verilator for this to get cleaned up.
>
> diff --git a/testbench/tb_top.sv b/testbench/tb_top.sv
> index 425d711..c9f6287 100644
> --- a/testbench/tb_top.sv
> +++ b/testbench/tb_top.sv
> @@ -686,7 +686,7 @@ end
> endtask
>
> `ifdef VERILATOR
> -`define DRAM(bank)
> rvtop.mem.Gen_dccm_enable.dccm.mem_bank[bank].ram.ram_core
> +`define DRAM(bank)
> rvtop.mem.Gen_dccm_enable.dccm.mem_bank[bank].genblk1.genblk1.genblk1.dccm.dccm_bank.ram_core
> `else
> `define DRAM(bank)
> rvtop.mem.Gen_dccm_enable.dccm.mem_bank[bank].dccm.dccm_bank.ram_core
> `endif
>
>
>
>> Hi Wilson
>>
>> Could you, please, check why do we need `ifdef VERILATOR in the design
>> files
>> as :
>>
>> design/lsu/elx2_lsu_dccm_mem.sv
>>
>> we are accessing memory arrays from TB via full path reference, but 4.20
>> verilator gives an error - can't find it,
>> So I had to pop up parameterizable memory one level up to make it
>> compile
>> ...
>>
>>
>> Thanks
>> Alex
